Patch Set 1:

This patch skips ALL nids if they packed with struct lnet_nid. My patch looks at the struct lnet_nid and uses it if its an older nid format. Is this patch for 2.12 LTS support since it doesn't have any idea about struct lnet_nid. The patch I worked out is indeed aimed for 2.15 LTS support.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> This patch skips ALL nids if they packed with struct lnet_nid. 

We never pack 4-byte nids in struct lnet_nid, so it makes no practical difference.

> Is this patch for 2.12 LTS support since it doesn't have any idea about struct lnet_nid. The patch I worked out is indeed aimed for 2.15 LTS support.

This patch is for anything that might need it.  It is deliberately as simple as possible to minimise any backporting risks.  There are always risks.  Smaller patches are easier to review.

Patch Set 1:

Ah I see the difference in approach. In your approach the complexity to handle both lnet_nid_t and struct lnet_nid is done on the MGS. My approach is that the mgc side handles both lnet_nid_t and struct lnet_nid_t. In that case the MGS side just deals with struct lnet_nid.
The reason for my approach is in the hope down the road we can phase out lnet_nid_t.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Talking with Andreas we discussed another approach which avoids having to patch older clients. I pushed a patch that only will push large NIDs on the MGS side only if the targets that register with the MGS report that it can support larger NIDs.

parents.subject: LU-16657 build: don't need to use zfs kmodtool on RHEL9.1

commit.subject: LU-10391 mgc: gracefully ignore unrecognised recovery logs
commit.message:
LU-10391 mgc: gracefully ignore unrecognised recovery logs

Incoming recovery logs with unrecognised entries should not be a fatal
error.  Providing the size information looks sensible we skip the
entry and move on to the next, rather than aborting at that point.

The "datalen < 0" is unnecessary as we already check mne_length <
entry_len and datalen > entry_len.

This patch is suitable for backporting to stable releases to allow
some interoperability with newer releases that support IPv6 and other
large-nid protocols.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Mr NeilBrown <neilb@suse.de>
Change-Id: I62cacaf0d2bdbc5dc7ccc40844771613c297e0b1
